symptoms.symptom_name: Longstanding reducible right groin bulge (6 months), enlarging with Valsalva/standing, flat when supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes a pre-existing inguinal hernia as the anatomical substrate; the acute change is a complication of it.
A pre-existing reducible inguinal mass points to hernia rather than an intratesticular process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe groin pain immediately after heavy lifting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A precipitating rise in intra-abdominal pressure forcing more bowel through the defect is the classic trigger for acute incarceration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute irreducibility — the lump is stuck, rock-hard and will not reduce with positioning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of reducibility in a previously reducible hernia defines incarceration and is the pivotal change from benign to emergency.
Irreducibility with severe tenderness is the precursor to and warning of strangulation.

symptoms.symptom_name: New nausea and one episode of non-bilious vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Indicates bowel involvement and early mechanical obstruction of the entrapped loop.
Nausea/vomiting also accompany torsion, so it does not discriminate between the two emergencies.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reddened skin over the mass and diaphoresis/clamminess (patient-observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overlying skin change and systemic upset raise concern that entrapped contents are becoming ischaemic; patient-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, exquisitely tender, irreducible mass protruding through the superficial inguinal ring toward the scrotum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective palpation confirming an irreducible tender inguinal mass — sealed clinician examination finding; the AI Doctor infers incarceration from the patient's report, not from palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent bowel sounds over the mass with abdominal distension and mild generalised discomfort

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auscultatory/examination evidence of mechanical obstruction — sealed clinician-only finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~105) with mildly elevated pain-mediated blood pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective tachycardia reflecting pain and early systemic response — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers systemic upset from the patient reporting feeling sweaty and unwell, not a measured vital sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unable to stand or walk because of pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marks severity and, with living alone, drives the need for ambulance rather than self-transport.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden pain relief with a darkening/dusky lump or dark foul vomiting (not yet present)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paradoxical loss of pain with a dusky lump or faeculent vomiting signals established bowel death/perforation — a further-deterioration marker to warn the patient/paramedics about.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute incarceration of a known hernia
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: incarcerated inguinal hernia with impending strangulation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A known reducible hernia + sudden pain after lifting + new irreducibility + vomiting is the recognisable emergency constellation that should trigger immediate escalation on history alone.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate in-person surgical assessment (examination of the hernia and abdomen)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only hands-on assessment can gauge reducibility, tenderness and bowel viability and decide reduction vs emergency operation — the AI Doctor should arrange this via emergency escalation, not attempt it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency bloods (FBC, CRP, lactate, electrolytes) and abdominal/groin imaging (ultrasound or CT)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Hospital work-up to confirm obstruction/strangulation and guide surgery; unavailable via telehealth and not to be fabricated.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral analgesia / antiemetic prescribed remotely by the AI Doctor
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient must be kept nil by mouth for likely imminent general anaesthesia; the AI must not mint a drug dose. Analgesia/antiemesis are in-person clinician acts.
